page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an arm64-based general-purpose server instance powered by the t2a family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 192 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. There is no bundled local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.

Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong multi-core capabilities, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 87022.37 and high-throughput Redis SET operations reaching 61.65 million operations per second. Single-core performance is more moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1110.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1813.29. Memory bandwidth is stable, recording 181.23 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 78.56 GB/sec for large-block write operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-48 leverages the ARM architecture to deliver high physical core density and dedicated compute resources without the overhead of hyperthreading. This makes it highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and database operations. However, it exhibits weaker performance in single-threaded tasks and large-model LLM text generation, making it less optimal for heavy sequential processing or complex AI inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an arm64 general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 architecture running at 3.0 GHz, paired with 192 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 87022.37 and Redis SET throughput of 61.65 million operations per second, though single-core metrics are comparatively moderate. This server is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, including web serving, database operations, and software compilation, offering a cost-effective alternative to traditional x86 architectures for parallelized tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-48 is an arm64 server with 48 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 192 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for web serving and database workloads.

tagline: An arm64 general-purpose server delivering 48 dedicated physical cores for highly efficient multi-threaded workloads and database operations.

192 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Strong multi-core performance with 87022.37 stress-ng score

Best suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
